Farn Pollrtcnl Pnrcucrs CouvttssloN
P.O. Bor lt07 . ll8 J Strcet 'Sacrirtnento. CA 951't ll-01.l07
(916) -lll-.5660 ' Fax (916) 322-01t86
February 9,2001
Grace Vallejo, Planning Commissioner
City of Delano Planning Commission
1608 18th Avenue
Delano, CA93215
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-021
Dear Ms. Vallejo:
This letter is in r*ponse to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").'
QUESTION
May you participate in a Planning Commission recommendation on an application
for arurexation submitted by your employer, the Delano High School District?
CONCLUSION
Yes. You may participate in this decision since you do not have an eccnomlc
interest in the Delano High School District, a govemment agency.
FACTS
You are a Planning Commissioner for the City of Delano. It has come to your
attention that the local high school District is apptying to the Planning Commission for
review and recommendation on their application for arurexation of property for their
second high school. You are a fuIl-time classified ernployee for the Delano High School
District as an Account Clerk II in charge of accounts payable.
' Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18996, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.


File No. A-01-021
Page No. 2
AITALYSN
The primary purpose for the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act is to ensure
that public offi.iuti, whither elected or appointed, perform their duties in an impartial
***.r, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or the financial interests of
personswho have supported them. (Section 81001(b).) In furtherance of this goal,
^Section s7100 of the Act prohibits apublic official from making, participating in making,
or othenvise using his or her official position to influence a govemmental decision in
which the official has a financial interest.
Determining whether a conflict of interest exists under Section 87100 of the Act
requires analysis oIth" following questions :2
Are you a public official?
As a member of the Delano Planning Commission, you are a "member, officer,
employee, or consultant of a state or local government agency" and, therefore, are subject
to the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act. (Section 82048; Regulation 18701(a).)
Are you making, participating in making, or influencing a governmental decision?
The Act provides the following:
o A public official "makes a govemmental decision" when the official,
acting within the authority of his or her office or position, votes on a
matter, obligates or commits his or her agency to any course of action,
or enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of his or her
agency. (Section 87100; Regulation 18702.1')
o A public official "participates in making a govemmental decision"
when, acting within the authority of his or her position and without
significant substantive or intervening review, the official negotiates,
advises or makes recommendations to the decisionmaker regarding the
govemmental decision. (Section 87 1 00; Regulation 187 02.2 -)
o A public official is attempting to use his or her official position to
.,influence a govemmental decision" before his or her own agency if,
for the purpose of influencing, the official contacts or appears before
any member, officer, employee, or consultant of his or her agency.
(Section 87 1 00; Regulation 187 02.3.)
Under these provisions, you will o'make a govemmental decision" if you vote to
recommend the application for annexation submitted by the Delano High School District.
' These questions are based on the Act's eight step conflict-of-interest analysis provided at
Regulation 187006). The Commission document "Can I Vote? Conflicts of Interest Overyiew" explains
these steps and is enclosed for your information.


File No. 4-01-021
Page No. 3
Additionally, you will "participate in making" or "influence" a governmental decision if
you perform any of the actions detailed above with respect to this decision.
What are your economic interests - 
the possible sources of a financial conflict of
interest?
You have inquired and provided facts only regarding your employer. For
purposes of this letter, we assume that you have no other economic interests relevant to
the governmental decisions about which you inquire.
A public official has an economic interest in any person from whom he/she has
received income aggregating $500 within 12 months prior to the time when the relevant
governmental decision is made. (Section 87103(c); Regulation I8703.3(a).) However,
"income" does not include salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem received
from a state, local, or federal govemment agency. (Section 82030(bX2).) Consequently,
you do not have an economic interest in the Delano High School District, a local
government agency, and will not have a conflict of interest in the decision about which
you have inquired.
We conclude our analysis here since the facts you have provided do not suggest
that the subsequent steps of the conflict of interest analysis are applicable to your
situation. If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Acting General Counsel
,,$cka
Natalie
I
ocanegra
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

interest in the Delano High School District, a govemment agency.

QUESTION: May you participate in a Planning Commission recommendation on an application
for arurexation submitted by your employer, the Delano High School District?

CONCLUSION: Yes. You may participate in this decision since you do not have an eccnomlc
interest in the Delano High School District, a govemment agency.
